patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "okay michael so i see in here that you're here because you're experiencing some symptoms that your pcp was concerned about i also see that he ordered a ct so could you tell me what what started what happened why do you think you are here"] hi doctor how are you

[Clinician asked: "hi i'm great thanks for asking"] well so yeah i just happened to have gone in for my regular visit and i just explained to my pcp that you know what i've been having some back pain it's usually on the left and you know he asked some questions and he said i should come see you

[Clinician asked: "alright yeah back pain especially like that like was it sharp could you tell me what kind of pain it was"] well yeah some days it was sharp some days it was n't some days it moves some days it does n't but it's mostly on the left
[Clinician asked: "okay"] that's
[Clinician asked: "alright"] yeah

[Clinician asked: "unloving the spectrum that we have right here let's see if we can dig down and get some details well a couple of things i wan na ask have you been a bit more tired than lately"] no not recall
[Clinician asked: "alright how about your appetite are you hungry or or not as hungry"] i i would say about his that that's pretty much unchanged
[Clinician asked: "okay your appetite is unchanged alright what about your water consumption"] that i'm sorry what did you say

[Clinician asked: "no it's fine how about your water consumption"] yes but i've been drinking a lot more water and i do realize that i i wake up sometimes during the night to you know use the restroom to pee more than i normally do
[Clinician asked: "okay so we have noticed an increase in urine output okay how do you have like any normal activities are you playing any sports"] yeah i ride i ride a bike every so often
[Clinician asked: "okay"] so good yeah
[Clinician asked: "okay and are you like watching any other sports like did you watch march madness"] no sorry
[Clinician asked: "be still my heart okay that's fine i'll forgive you so"] thank you
[Clinician asked: "okay but how is school like are you like are are things going well like are you enjoying like your your time there"] yeah school is actually going well gotten good grades
[Clinician asked: "nice"] although my my physics teachers socks cheese

[Clinician asked: "they usually do anybody who chooses to engage in physics for the rest of their life i question their choices it's fine okay but are you when you're like while you are at school like has anybody been sick i know like with you know covid has been going around but like have you noticed anybody like tha"] sick all the time everybody is sick these days
[Clinician asked: "alright"] so

[Clinician asked: "but what about at home how about your siblings are any of them sick"] most of them are healthy yeah most of them are healthy although you know i have a brother who smokes i have told him not to smoke he does n't listen
[Clinician asked: "mm-hmm"] house told not to smoke at the house but you know anyway

[Clinician asked: "okay so there is a smoker at home you know that does that does affect you so we might have to have a conversation with your brother and anybody else but it's good to know that people are n't sick i do see here though that you have kind of a a history of recurrent strep like you you seem to be having"] yeah strep and bfs i'm not quite sure what it is just seems to come on you know i get this sore throat and fibroid i'm told i have strep
[Clinician asked: "okay"] sometimes you know my doctor knows what he is doing strep everyday strep everyday but anyway if he says i have strep then i have strep

[Clinician asked: "i okay i respect that and i'm glad that you you listen to him at the end of the day that's always a good thing alright so now that we've gotten like kind of a lot of the history out of the way i do want to do my physical exam is that okay"] sure

[Clinician asked: "alright so when i look in your throat like i'm not noticing any erythema no swelling your tonsils are midline so those look good when i'm feeling here on your neck i'm not appreciating any like lymphadenopathy no cervical adenopathy and like i wan na check those things because i just wan na make sur"] no
[Clinician asked: "okay alright great no tenderness on palpation when i press on your abdomen are you feeling any pain"] no

[Clinician asked: "alright no rebound no guarding that's great okay so based on my physical exam we are gon na have a couple conversations one that ct that your your primary care doctor the the one that you you question his judgment it is showing a couple things that we are a little concerned about it could be that th"] how much are all these tests going to cost
[Clinician asked: "they are going to be free ninety nine because your mom is paying for them"] okay that's that's good to know
[Clinician asked: "yes"] and when do i when do i have to do those tests

[Clinician asked: "i would prefer you to get them done as soon as possible so when you walk out of this room there is a a blood lab right next door i need you to go ahead and do some of those draws some of them i will need you to not have eaten we are gon na do them it's called fasting and you can make sure that you h"] okay and i'm not sure i will be able to do that today because i have a class right after this
[Clinician asked: "mm-hmm"] and right after that i have to go to work
[Clinician asked: "mm-hmm"] you know if i told you i'm about changing jobs did i

[Clinician asked: "no you did n't mention that but one of the cool things about being a doctor is i can write you a note so you're gon na do it and then you can go to the class and we could figure it but like it's really cool that you're changing jobs i'm happy for you i hope you know that like it's always great to be"] well yeah this new job there is no insurance and my old boss sucks so i'm hoping i'll get a new boss and you know hopefully some free food with with this ut because it's had a restaurant so we'll we'll see how that goes

[Clinician asked: "we might have to have a conversation about the free food at the restaurant if we do n't get some of these infections under control because your salt intake is about to be very important okay"] got it
[Clinician asked: "alright okay so do you have any more questions before i send my nurse in"] do i need to get a shot today
[Clinician asked: "no shots today just stopped"] okay and when do i have to come back for the results
[Clinician asked: "i need you to come back in a week"] okay can we do that over the phone by the way
[Clinician asked: "we could if if your results are normal but if we need to have additional conversations i'm gon na need you to come back"] excellent
[Clinician asked: "okay"] thank you very much
gold_note:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Back pain.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he has a frequent history of strep throat with 4 episodes within the last 5 months.

SOCIAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he is a student. He also mentions that he rides his bike every so often and that he is in the process of switching jobs. His new job will be at a restaurant.

FAMILY HISTORY

Patient reports that his siblings are mostly healthy. He does have a brother who smokes while at home.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Denies fatigue or change in appetite.
Genitourinary: Reports increased urine output.
Musculoskeletal: Reports left-side back pain.
Endocrine: Reports increased water consumption

PHYSICAL EXAM

Ears, Nose, Mouth, and Throat
- Examination of Mouth: Normal.
- Examination of Throat: Pharynx without erythema, swelling, or exudate. Tonsils are midline.

Neck
- General Examination: Neck is supple without lymphadenopathy. No cervical adenopathy.

Respiratory
- Auscultation of Lungs: No wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.

Cardiovascular
- Auscultation of Heart: No murmurs, gallops or rubs.

Gastrointestinal
- Examination of Abdomen: No tenderness. No rebound or guarding.

Musculoskeletal
- Examination: No back tenderness with palpation.

Hematology/Lymphatic/Immunology
- Palpation: No enlarged lymph nodes.

RESULTS

Previous CT scan is reviewed and demonstrates evidence of swelling potentially related to glomerulonephritis.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Glomerulonephritis.
- Medical Reasoning: The patient presents today with a complaint of back pain, although this was not present on exam today with palpation. He also has a history of recurrent strep throat and a recent CT scan with concerns of possible glomerulonephritis.
- Patient Education and Counseling: The patient was recommended to watch his salt intake.
- Medical Treatment: We will refer him to an ENT for further evaluation and treatment. We will also order a lipid panel, UA, CMP, CBC, vitamin D, and a TSH. A work note was provided today as well.

Patient Agreements: The patient understands and agrees with the recommended medical treatment plan.

INSTRUCTIONS

He will follow up in 1 week via telehealth, however if the results are abnormal this will need to be an in-person visit.

naive.response_text:
Michael, thank you for sharing your concerns with me. I understand that the back pain you're experiencing has been quite uncomfortable, and I appreciate you taking the time to describe your symptoms in detail. It's important that we investigate this further to ensure we're addressing it properly, which is why the CT scan was recommended. I want to assure you that we'll handle the testing process as smoothly as possible, considering your busy schedule. If the timing is an issue, we can explore alternative arrangements to make it work for you.

Regarding the increased urination, we'll keep a close eye on that as well. It's good that you're staying hydrated, but we'll monitor any changes. Your history of strep throat seems to be well-managed, given how well you're doing in school. I'm glad you're handling everything so well. Please don't hesitate to ask any more questions before you leave, and we'll make sure to keep you informed every step of the way.

strong_prompt.response_text:
Thank you for sharing all that with me, Michael. It makes sense that you're feeling a bit concerned about the tests and your schedule. I can see how important it is for you to manage your time between class, work, and taking care of your health. Let's work together to make this as smooth as possible for you.

The tests we're recommending, like the CT scan and blood work, are important to help us understand what's going on with your back pain. These imaging studies can give us clearer insights, and the blood work can check for any underlying issues that might be contributing to your symptoms [PMID ?]. I understand that waiting for results can be stressful, but they'll help guide the next steps in your care.

Given your schedule, I'll make sure to prioritize getting the necessary tests done efficiently. If the timing is an issue, we can explore options to accommodate your classes and work. It's also good to keep an eye on your health, especially with the job change and potential changes in your routine.

We'll plan to follow up in a week to review the results. If everything looks good, we can discuss any further steps over the phone. If there's anything else we need to address, I'll make sure you know when you need to come back. Please don't hesitate to ask any questions you might have before you leave.
